generated_code:
# Test: Property inheritance with virtual/override, polymorphic dispatch
# Tests: Function-style and auto-properties across class hierarchies

class Shape:
    _color: str
    
    def __init__(self, color: str):
        self._color = color
    
    # Virtual computed property
    @virtual
    property get color(self) -> str:
        return self._color
    
    @virtual
    property set color(self, value: str):
        self._color = value
    
    # Virtual computed property
    @virtual
    property get area(self) -> float:
        return 0.0
    
    # Virtual read-only computed property
    @virtual
    property get description(self) -> str:
        return f"A {self.color} shape with area {self.area:.2f}"

class Rectangle(Shape):
    _width: float
    _height: float
    
    def __init__(self, color: str, width: float, height: float):
        super().__init__(color)
        self._width = width
        self._height = height
    
    # Override computed property
    @override
    property get area(self) -> float:
        return self._width * self._height
    
    # Add new computed property
    property get diagonal(self) -> float:
        return pow(self._width * self._width + self._height * self._height, 0.5)
    
    # Override description to include dimensions
    @override
    property get description(self) -> str:
        return f"A {self.color} rectangle {self._width:.1f}x{self._height:.1f}, area={self.area:.2f}, diagonal={self.diagonal:.2f}"

class Circle(Shape):
    _radius: float
    
    def __init__(self, color: str, radius: float):
        super().__init__(color)
        self._radius = radius
    
    # Override color with custom setter behavior
    @override
    property get color(self) -> str:
        return self._color
    
    @override
    property set color(self, value: str):
        # Validate color
        if value == "":
            self._color = "unknown"
        else:
            self._color = value
    
    # Override computed property
    @override
    property get area(self) -> float:
        return 3.14159 * self._radius * self._radius
    
    # Override description
    @override
    property get description(self) -> str:
        return f"A {self.color} circle with radius {self._radius:.1f}, area={self.area:.2f}"

def print_all_descriptions(shapes: list[Shape]):
    for i in range(len(shapes)):
        desc = shapes[i].description
        print(desc)

def main():
    shapes: list[Shape] = []
    rect = Rectangle("blue", 3.0, 4.0)
    circle = Circle("red", 5.0)
    
    # Test polymorphic property dispatch
    shapes.append(rect)
    shapes.append(circle)
    
    # Test overriding color
    rect.color = "green"
    circle.color = ""  # Should become "unknown"
    
    # Print colors
    print(rect.color)
    print(circle.color)
    
    # Print areas
    print(rect.area)
    print(circle.area)
    
    # Print diagonal (Rectangle-specific)
    print(rect.diagonal)
    
    # Print all descriptions - should use overridden properties
    print_all_descriptions(shapes)
expected_output:
green
unknown
12.0
78.53975
5.0

A unknown circle with radius 5.0, area=78.54
